name: Claude
description: Advanced AI for writing, research, strategy, and content creation
why: Claude excels at nuanced writing and structured thinking. For research and content strategy, it produces cleaner, more detailed output than most alternatives — and it follows complex instructions without drifting.

prompts.prompt:
I want to start a YouTube channel about [YOUR TOPIC]. Help me find a specific niche within this topic that:
1. Has clear audience demand (describe the audience)
2. Has less competition than the main topic
3. I can realistically cover with [YOUR BACKGROUND/EXPERIENCE]

Give me 5 niche options, ranked by opportunity. For each one: who the audience is, what makes it underserved, and 3 example video titles.
steps: Go to claude.ai and sign in
Click 'New conversation'
Replace [YOUR TOPIC] with your channel topic
Replace [YOUR BACKGROUND/EXPERIENCE] with your relevant skills
Paste the prompt and hit Enter
Ask Claude to go deeper on whichever niche resonates most

prompts.prompt:
Create a 90-day content calendar for a YouTube channel about [YOUR NICHE]. I want to post [FREQUENCY] times per week.

For each video include:
- Title (optimized for search)
- Hook (first 30 seconds idea)
- Main angle (what makes this different from other videos on this topic)
- Estimated length
- Best day to post

Group videos into monthly themes so the channel builds momentum.
steps: Open claude.ai
Replace [YOUR NICHE] with your specific channel topic
Replace [FREQUENCY] with how often you'll post (1, 2, or 3 times)
Paste and send
Ask Claude to expand on any video ideas that excite you
Export the calendar to a spreadsheet or Notion

prompts.prompt:
Write a YouTube channel description for [YOUR CHANNEL NAME], a channel about [YOUR TOPIC].

The description should:
- Hook viewers in the first 2 sentences (this is what shows in search)
- Include these keywords naturally: [LIST 3-5 KEYWORDS]
- Tell viewers exactly what they'll get and how often
- End with a call to subscribe

Keep it under 200 words. Also give me 3 alternative opening lines I can test.
steps: Open claude.ai
Replace [YOUR CHANNEL NAME] with your channel name
Replace [YOUR TOPIC] with your niche
List 3-5 keywords you found in your niche research
Paste and send
Use the description in YouTube Studio > Customization > Basic Info

name: ChatGPT
description: OpenAI's AI assistant for brainstorming, writing, and analysis
why: ChatGPT's browsing capability lets it pull real-time data for trend analysis. Use it when you need current information or want to cross-check what Claude produces.

prompts.prompt:
Search for the top trending topics in [YOUR NICHE] on YouTube right now. For each trend:
- What's the trend (explain simply)
- Why is it trending now (context)
- How long will it likely last
- Video angle I could take to cover this trend uniquely

Give me 10 trends ranked from hottest to most evergreen.
steps: Go to chat.openai.com
Make sure you're on GPT-4 with browsing enabled
Replace [YOUR NICHE] with your topic
Send the prompt
Ask it to find specific examples of viral videos in these trends

prompts.prompt:
Write 10 YouTube video hooks for a video titled '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]'. Each hook should be under 30 seconds when spoken aloud (about 60 words).

Mix these styles:
- Bold claim hook
- Story hook
- Question hook
- Surprising fact hook
- Relatable problem hook

Make them punchy, specific, and designed to stop someone from scrolling.
steps: Open ChatGPT
Replace [YOUR VIDEO TITLE] with your actual video title
Send the prompt
Pick the 3 hooks that feel most authentic to you
Test different hooks in your first few videos and track retention

name: Midjourney
description: AI image generation for thumbnails, channel art, and promotional visuals
why: Midjourney produces the most photorealistic and aesthetically consistent images of any AI image generator. For YouTube thumbnails that need to stop the scroll, Midjourney's quality edge matters.

prompts.prompt: Close-up portrait of a [GENDER] person, [AGE RANGE], [EXPRESSION: shocked/excited/curious], looking directly at camera, vibrant [COLOR PALETTE] background, professional YouTube thumbnail style, high contrast, sharp focus, studio lighting, 16:9 composition --ar 16:9 --style raw --v 6
steps: Go to midjourney.com and log in
Click 'Create' to open the image generator
Replace bracketed variables with your specifics
Paste the prompt and generate
Click on the image you like most, then upscale it (U1-U4)
Download the high-res version
Add text overlay in Canva or Photoshop

prompts.prompt: [YOUR AESTHETIC: e.g. minimalist modern / cozy warm / bold energetic] YouTube channel banner, [YOUR TOPIC] theme, wide cinematic composition, [YOUR COLOR 1] and [YOUR COLOR 2] color scheme, space on right side for text overlay, professional quality, 2560x1440 resolution style --ar 16:9 --v 6
steps: Open Midjourney
Describe your channel's aesthetic and colors
Generate 4 options and choose the best
Upscale your chosen image
Download and bring into Canva to add your channel name and schedule

name: Canva AI
description: AI-powered design tool for logos, banners, thumbnails, and social graphics
why: Canva AI combines design templates with AI generation. For creators who aren't designers, it's the fastest path from 'I need a logo' to a file you can actually upload — without needing Photoshop skills.

prompts.prompt: Create a YouTube channel logo for [YOUR CHANNEL NAME] about [YOUR TOPIC]. Style: [STYLE: minimalist / bold / playful / professional]. Primary color: [YOUR COLOR]. Include: just the logo mark or icon, no wordmark needed. The logo should look good at 800x800px (YouTube profile picture) and be recognizable when small.
steps: Go to canva.com and sign in
Click 'Create a design' → Logo
Use Magic Design: describe your channel and style
Or search templates for your category
Customize colors to match your brand
Download as PNG with transparent background
Upload to YouTube Studio > Customization > Branding

prompts.prompt: Design a YouTube thumbnail template for [YOUR CHANNEL NAME]. The template should have: space for a main image on [LEFT/RIGHT], bold title text area on the [OTHER SIDE], [YOUR CHANNEL COLOR] accent elements, and your channel logo in the corner. Make it reusable — I'll swap the main image and title for each video.
steps: Open Canva, click 'Create a design' → YouTube Thumbnail
Search templates for your style or use Magic Design
Set your brand colors in Brand Kit
Create placeholders for the image and text
Save as a template
Duplicate for each new video and swap the content

name: Runway
description: AI video generation from text descriptions — create professional video clips without filming
why: Runway Gen-3 creates broadcast-quality video from text alone. The free tier gives you enough credits for a channel intro. No video editing experience needed — if you can describe it, Runway can render it.

prompts.prompt: Create a 5-second YouTube channel intro animation: [YOUR SCENE DESCRIPTION — e.g. warm kitchen with fresh ingredients / dramatic mountain landscape / bright office with tech gadgets], camera slowly zooming in, soft professional lighting, cinematic quality. Style: [YOUR AESTHETIC: cozy and warm / bold and modern / clean and minimal]. Text overlay fades in at the end: '[YOUR CHANNEL NAME]'
steps: Go to runwayml.com and sign up (free tier available)
Click 'Create' → Gen-3 Alpha Turbo
Replace the bracketed description with your scene
Set duration to 5 seconds
Click Generate (takes about 30 seconds)
If you don't love the result, click Regenerate or refine the prompt
Download the MP4 and add to your video editor as the intro

prompts.prompt: [SCENE: e.g. steam rising from a cooking pot / code scrolling on dark screens / hands sketching on paper], slow motion, soft natural lighting, shallow depth of field, cinematic 24fps feel, no people, atmospheric and calming, perfect as video background
steps: Open Runway Gen-3
Describe the background scene you need
Generate 4-second clips
Download and use as B-roll footage in your editor
Layer your voiceover or screen recording on top

name: ElevenLabs
description: AI voice generation — create natural voiceovers and narration from text
why: ElevenLabs produces the most natural-sounding AI voices available. For creators who don't want to record their own voiceover, or who want a consistent brand voice, it's the clear leader. Free tier gives you 10 minutes/month.

prompts.prompt:
[PASTE YOUR VIDEO SCRIPT HERE]

---
Voice direction: Speak this as a [TONE: warm and friendly / authoritative expert / excited and energetic] host. Pace: [PACE: conversational / measured and clear / upbeat]. Emphasize key phrases naturally. This is for a YouTube video about [YOUR TOPIC].
steps: Go to elevenlabs.io and create a free account
Click 'Text to Speech'
Browse voices and preview a few — pick one that fits your brand
Paste your script in the text box
Adjust stability (higher = more consistent) and clarity
Click Generate
Download the MP3 and import into your video editor

prompts.prompt: Welcome to [YOUR CHANNEL NAME] — where [YOUR VALUE PROPOSITION in one sentence]. I'm [YOUR NAME OR PERSONA], and every [POSTING FREQUENCY] I show you [WHAT YOU TEACH]. Subscribe and hit the bell so you never miss a video.
steps: Open ElevenLabs Text to Speech
Fill in your channel details in the template
Choose a voice that matches your brand personality
Generate the audio
Use this 15-second clip at the start of every video for brand consistency

name: Cursor
description: AI-powered code editor for building websites, tools, and automations
why: Cursor lets non-programmers build real software. If your dream involves a website, an app, or any automation, Cursor is the fastest way to get from idea to working code — even if you've never coded before.

prompts.prompt:
Build me a simple landing page for [YOUR PROJECT NAME]. It should have:
- A hero section with headline: '[YOUR HEADLINE]'
- A features section listing: [LIST 3 FEATURES]
- An email signup form
- Dark background, [YOUR COLOR] accent color
- Mobile responsive

Use plain HTML, CSS, and minimal JavaScript. No frameworks.
steps: Download Cursor from cursor.sh
Open Cursor and create a new folder for your project
Press Cmd+I (Mac) or Ctrl+I (Windows) to open the AI composer
Paste the prompt with your specifics filled in
Cursor will write the code — review it and ask for changes
Press the Run button to preview in your browser
Deploy to Vercel or Netlify for free

prompts.prompt: Write a Python script that [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT TO AUTOMATE — e.g. 'downloads all images from a folder of URLs', 'renames video files based on a spreadsheet', 'sends a Slack message when a file is added to a folder']. Make it beginner-friendly with comments explaining each step.
steps: Open Cursor
Create a new file called script.py
Describe your automation in plain English in the AI chat
Cursor writes the script
Ask it to add error handling and comments
Run the script and ask Cursor to fix any errors

name: Gamma
description: AI presentation builder — creates beautiful decks from a text prompt in seconds
why: Gamma produces investor-grade presentations from a text prompt in under 2 minutes. It handles layout, design, and visual hierarchy automatically. For pitching sponsors or investors, it saves a day of Keynote work.

prompts.prompt:
Create a YouTube sponsorship media kit for [YOUR CHANNEL NAME]. Include:
- Channel overview: [NUMBER] subscribers, [YOUR NICHE] audience
- Audience demographics: [AGE RANGE], [INTERESTS]
- Content types: [WHAT YOU CREATE]
- Sponsorship packages: Pre-roll (30 sec), Mid-roll (60 sec), Dedicated video
- Pricing: [YOUR RATES or leave TBD]
- Past performance: average [YOUR VIEWS] views per video
- Contact: [YOUR EMAIL]

Make it look professional and data-driven.
steps: Go to gamma.app and sign up (free tier available)
Click 'Create new' → 'Generate'
Paste the prompt with your channel stats
Choose a theme that matches your brand colors
Gamma generates the full deck in seconds
Edit slide by slide to add your real data
Export as PDF or share via link

prompts.prompt: Create a 1-year business plan presentation for [YOUR VENTURE]. Sections: Executive Summary, Problem, Solution, Target Market, Revenue Streams ([LIST THEM]), 12-Month Roadmap, Financial Projections (conservative case), Team, and Next Steps. Audience: potential investors/partners.
steps: Open gamma.app
Use the prompt to generate your deck
Review each slide and refine the messaging
Add your actual numbers in the financial section
Share via link for investor meetings

name: Perplexity
description: AI-powered research engine with real-time web search and cited sources
why: Perplexity searches the current web and gives you cited sources — not training data from a year ago. For market research, trend analysis, and competitor research, it's more trustworthy than ChatGPT because you can verify every claim.

prompts.prompt:
What is the current state of the [YOUR NICHE] market in [YEAR]? I want to know:
1. Market size and growth rate
2. Top 5 competitors or key players
3. Recent trends or shifts in the last 6 months
4. Underserved gaps or opportunities
5. Consumer pain points

Cite your sources for each point.
steps: Go to perplexity.ai
Paste the research prompt with your niche filled in
Review the cited sources — click through to verify key claims
Follow up with more specific questions based on what you find
Use the findings to position your channel or business uniquely

prompts.prompt:
Research the top YouTube channels about [YOUR NICHE]. For each major channel:
- Subscriber count and growth rate
- What content performs best for them
- Their unique angle or positioning
- Comments or audience complaints (what they're missing)

Then tell me: what gap exists that a new channel could fill?
steps: Open Perplexity
Run the competitor research prompt
For channels Perplexity names, go to YouTube to verify stats
Pay special attention to the 'gap' analysis
Use the gap to define your unique channel angle

name: CapCut
description: AI video editor with auto-captions, templates, and one-click enhancements
why: CapCut's AI auto-captions are faster and more accurate than most alternatives, and it's free. For YouTube Shorts and social clips, it has the best template library. No learning curve required.

prompts.prompt:
I need to edit a [LENGTH]-minute YouTube video about [YOUR TOPIC]. The raw footage has:
- [DESCRIBE FOOTAGE: talking head / screen recording / cooking demo / etc.]
- Background noise issues: [YES/NO]
- Multiple takes I need to cut together

Give me a step-by-step editing workflow optimized for CapCut, including which AI features to use for efficiency.
steps: Download CapCut (desktop or mobile)
Import your raw footage
Use 'Auto Captions' to transcribe your video
Edit by deleting caption segments (removes the video too)
Use 'Enhance Voice' to clean up audio
Add B-roll from your Runway clips
Export at 1080p for YouTube

prompts.prompt: Take my [LENGTH]-minute YouTube video and help me identify the best 3 moments to turn into YouTube Shorts (under 60 seconds each). For each moment: timestamp, why it works as a short, suggested hook text to add at the top, and caption style recommendation.
steps: Open CapCut and import your full video
Use the Smart Reframe or Clip feature
Identify high-energy moments: strong reactions, key tips, surprising moments
Export each short at 9:16 vertical format
Post Shorts 2-3 days after your main video goes up

name: Notion AI
description: AI-enhanced notes and documents for planning, SOPs, and content organization
why: Notion AI combines a flexible workspace with AI writing. For content creators, it's the best place to manage your content calendar, video scripts, and brand guidelines in one place — with AI that writes inside your existing notes.

prompts.prompt:
Build me a YouTube content management system in Notion for [YOUR CHANNEL NAME]. I need:
- A video pipeline tracker (Idea → Script → Filmed → Edited → Scheduled → Published)
- A content calendar view by month
- A video idea database with columns for: Title, Niche Tag, Hook idea, Status, Priority
- A script template I can duplicate for each video

Describe the exact database structure and properties to set up.
steps: Create a free Notion account at notion.so
Click 'Add a page' → 'AI' to use Notion AI
Paste the prompt to generate the structure
Create each database following the described structure
Add your existing video ideas to the database
Review and update your pipeline every week

prompts.prompt:
Write a video script outline for a YouTube video titled '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]'. Structure:
- Hook (0-30 sec): [HOOK TYPE: bold claim / story / question]
- Context (30-60 sec): why this matters to the viewer
- Main content (broken into 3-5 clear sections)
- Transitions between sections
- CTA (last 30 sec): subscribe + what to watch next

Target length: [TARGET MINUTES] minutes. Tone: [YOUR TONE].
steps: Open Notion and create a new page
Activate Notion AI with the space bar or /AI
Paste the script prompt with your video details
Review the outline and expand each section
Record using the outline as your guide

name: Figma AI
description: AI-assisted UI design tool for creating professional visual content and mockups
why: Figma's AI features generate UI components and layouts from text. For anyone building a product, app, or website alongside their content creation, Figma AI dramatically reduces the design-to-development gap.

prompts.prompt:
Design a brand style guide for [YOUR BRAND NAME] in Figma. Include:
- Color palette: primary [COLOR], secondary [COLOR], accent [COLOR], neutral grays
- Typography: heading font, body font, and when to use each
- Logo usage rules
- Button and UI element styles
- Do/don't examples for brand consistency

This will be the reference for all my content visuals.
steps: Create a free Figma account
Open a new file and name it '[Your Brand] Style Guide'
Use Figma AI (press Cmd+K or look for the AI icon) to generate components
Create color swatches and label them with hex codes
Choose and set your Google Fonts
Share this file with anyone who creates content for you

prompts.prompt: Design a YouTube thumbnail in Figma for a video titled '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]'. The thumbnail should have: [YOUR MAIN VISUAL description], bold white text '[YOUR THUMBNAIL TEXT]' with a dark drop shadow, [YOUR ACCENT COLOR] element for visual pop, and a small [YOUR LOGO] in the corner. Size: 1280x720px.
steps: Open Figma, create a new frame at 1280x720
Import your Midjourney or Canva background image
Add text with a strong contrast color
Use the Figma AI to generate matching design elements
Export as PNG at 2x resolution
Upload to YouTube when scheduling your video

name: Suno
description: AI music generation — create custom background music and jingles from text prompts
why: Suno generates full songs in 2 minutes from a text description. For channel intros, background music, and social clips, it eliminates the need for stock music licensing entirely. Free tier gives you 50 credits per day.

prompts.prompt: [GENRE: upbeat electronic / warm acoustic / cinematic orchestral / lo-fi hip hop], channel intro music, [MOOD: energetic and motivating / calm and focused / warm and welcoming], 10-15 seconds long, strong memorable melody, builds to a punchy end, no lyrics, professional quality, suitable for YouTube
steps: Go to suno.com and sign up for free
Click 'Create' and paste your music prompt
Choose 'Instrumental' to avoid lyrics
Generate 2 versions and pick the best
Download the MP3
Trim to exactly the length you need in CapCut or your editor

prompts.prompt: [GENRE] background music for a YouTube video about [YOUR TOPIC], [MOOD], no lyrics, consistent energy level (not too dynamic), loops cleanly, [BPM: slow 70bpm / medium 100bpm / upbeat 130bpm], professional production quality
steps: Open Suno and create an instrumental track
Describe the mood and energy you need
Generate multiple versions to find the right feel
Download and import to your video editor
Set the music volume to 10-15% of your voice level

name: DALL-E
description: OpenAI's image generator integrated directly into ChatGPT
why: DALL-E 3 is built into ChatGPT Plus, making it the most accessible AI image generator — no separate tool required. It's especially strong at following text instructions exactly and generating images with accurate text overlays.

prompts.prompt: Create a [STYLE: flat illustration / realistic photo / digital art] image for a YouTube video about '[YOUR TOPIC]'. The image should show [DESCRIBE THE CONCEPT OR SCENE]. Color palette: [YOUR COLORS]. Mood: [MOOD]. This will be used as a video thumbnail or social media graphic. Make it visually striking and clear at small sizes.
steps: Open ChatGPT (requires Plus subscription for DALL-E 3)
Click the image icon or just describe what you want
Paste your prompt with specifics filled in
ChatGPT will generate 1-2 options
Ask for variations: 'Make it more [ADJUSTMENT]'
Right-click to download the image

prompts.prompt: Create a social media graphic announcing a new YouTube video titled '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]'. Style: [STYLE]. Include space for text overlay. Background: [DESCRIBE]. The graphic should feel [MOOD] and appeal to [YOUR TARGET AUDIENCE]. Square format (1:1 ratio).
steps: Generate the base image in DALL-E via ChatGPT
Download the image
Open in Canva to add your video title text
Resize as needed for Instagram (1:1), Twitter (16:9), Story (9:16)
Post 1-2 days before your video goes live

name: Stable Diffusion
description: Open-source AI image generation with unlimited free generations via local run
why: Stable Diffusion runs locally on your computer for free — unlimited generations, no API costs. For high-volume visual needs like thumbnails and social graphics, it's the most cost-efficient option once set up.

prompts.prompt: [PRODUCT DESCRIPTION], professional product photography style, clean white or gradient background, soft studio lighting from [LEFT/RIGHT/TOP], sharp focus, commercial quality, no watermarks, 8k resolution aesthetic
steps: Use Stable Diffusion via Automatic1111 (local) or stablediffusionweb.com (browser)
Use a product-focused model like RealVisXL or DreamShaper
Set steps to 30, CFG to 7, resolution to 1024x1024
Generate your product prompt
Use inpainting to remove background or fix details
Download and use in your content

prompts.prompt: [SCENE DESCRIPTION], in the style of [ART STYLE: ukiyo-e woodblock print / watercolor painting / oil painting / comic book / cyberpunk digital art], masterpiece quality, detailed, vibrant colors, atmospheric lighting
steps: Open your Stable Diffusion interface
Choose a model that matches your art style
Paste the prompt and generate 4 images
Select the best one and upscale if needed
Use as visual content for your channel or social posts

name: Descript
description: Edit video by editing text — transcript-based editing, filler word removal, and AI voice overdub
why: Descript turns your video into a document you edit with a keyboard. Delete a word from the transcript and it deletes that moment from the video. It removes filler words ('um', 'uh') in one click. For interviews and talking-head content, it's 5x faster than timeline editing.

prompts.prompt:
I recorded a [LENGTH]-minute talking-head YouTube video about [YOUR TOPIC]. I need to:
1. Remove all filler words (um, uh, like, you know)
2. Cut the dead air/long pauses
3. Remove 3-4 rambling sections where I repeat myself
4. Add captions for accessibility

Give me the exact Descript workflow to do this in under 30 minutes.
steps: Upload your video to Descript
Wait for the auto-transcription (2-5 minutes)
Click 'Remove filler words' — review what it flags
Select and delete transcript sections you don't want
Use 'Find and replace' to find specific repeated phrases
Add captions from the existing transcript
Export the edited video as MP4

prompts.prompt: I mispronounced [WORD/PHRASE] 3 times in my video. I also said the wrong statistic at [TIMESTAMP] — I said [WRONG INFO] but it should be [CORRECT INFO]. Use Descript's Overdub feature to fix these without re-recording. What's the exact process?
steps: Upload your video and train Descript on your voice (takes 10 min setup)
Find the error in the transcript
Select the wrong text and type the correction
Descript generates your voice saying the correction
Review the overdubbed section — regenerate if it sounds off
Export the corrected video

name: Synthesia
description: AI video with realistic avatars — create presenter videos without filming yourself
why: Synthesia lets you create professional video with an AI avatar reading your script. For educational content, explainers, and training videos where you don't want to appear on camera, it's the fastest professional option.

prompts.prompt:
Write a 2-minute explainer video script for Synthesia about '[YOUR TOPIC]'. Format for a presenter avatar:
- Simple, clear sentences (avatar reads these exactly)
- No complex jargon (explain any technical terms)
- Pause indicators [PAUSE] where the avatar should pause
- Slide cue notes [SLIDE: description] for when to advance
- Friendly and professional tone
- End with a clear next step for the viewer
steps: Sign up at synthesia.io (free trial available)
Click 'Create video' → choose a template
Choose your AI avatar (50+ to pick from)
Paste your script into the text box
Select your avatar's language and accent
Add slides, backgrounds, and screen recordings
Click Generate — video is ready in minutes
Download MP4

name: Copy.ai
description: AI copywriting tool for marketing, ads, and social media content
why: Copy.ai specializes in short-form marketing copy — ad headlines, social captions, email subjects. Where Claude excels at long-form thinking, Copy.ai is faster for high-volume short copy with multiple variants.

prompts.prompt:
Write 10 Instagram and Twitter captions for a YouTube video titled '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]' about [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]. Include:
- 3 question-style captions to drive comments
- 3 value-focused captions highlighting what viewers learn
- 2 behind-the-scenes style captions
- 2 short punchy captions under 10 words

Add 5 relevant hashtags to each. Tone: [YOUR TONE].
steps: Go to copy.ai and sign in
Use the 'Social Media Captions' template or the Chat feature
Fill in your video details
Generate 10+ options
Pick the best 3-5 for each platform
Schedule them in Buffer or Later

prompts.prompt:
Write an email newsletter announcing my new YouTube video '[YOUR VIDEO TITLE]'. The email should:
- Subject line: [3 OPTIONS]
- Opening hook that creates curiosity
- 2-3 sentences on what they'll learn
- Clear CTA button text: [WATCH NOW / CHECK IT OUT / SEE THE VIDEO]
- Friendly sign-off

Keep it under 200 words. Tone: [YOUR BRAND TONE].
steps: Use Copy.ai's Email template
Fill in your video details
Generate the email draft
Paste into your email tool (Mailchimp, ConvertKit, etc.)
Send to your list 1-2 hours after the video goes live

name: Jasper
description: AI content platform for long-form writing, blogs, and marketing content at scale
why: Jasper excels at long-form content that maintains brand voice consistently. If you're writing multiple blog posts, articles, or a book alongside your video content, Jasper's brand voice training keeps everything consistent.

prompts.prompt:
Write a 1500-word SEO blog post titled '[YOUR BLOG TITLE]' targeting the keyword '[TARGET KEYWORD]'. Structure:
- Meta description (155 chars)
- Introduction with the keyword in first 100 words
- H2 sections covering: [LIST 4-5 SUBTOPICS]
- 1 ordered list or comparison table
- Conclusion with a CTA to watch the related YouTube video

Tone: [YOUR BRAND VOICE]. No fluff — every paragraph should add value.
steps: Open Jasper and choose the 'Blog Post' workflow
Input your keyword and title
Let Jasper create an outline and review it
Use Jasper to write section by section
Edit for your voice and add your specific examples
Publish and link to your YouTube video

name: Pika
description: AI video generation and animation from images or text — fast and creative
why: Pika is faster and cheaper than Runway with a more creative, stylized output. For social media clips, animated thumbnails, and quick video content, Pika's free tier is the best starting point before investing in Runway.

prompts.prompt: [SCENE DESCRIPTION — e.g. a coffee mug steaming on a wooden desk / hands typing on a laptop keyboard / colorful vegetables being chopped], [CAMERA MOTION: slow zoom in / pan left / static shot], [STYLE: cinematic / vibrant and colorful / moody dark], short 3-second clip, loopable, high quality
steps: Go to pika.art and sign up for free
Click 'Create' → Text to Video
Paste your scene description
Select duration (3-5 seconds is ideal for social)
Generate and download
Use in TikTok, Instagram Reels, or YouTube Shorts

prompts.prompt: Animate this image: [DESCRIBE YOUR IMAGE]. Motion: [DESCRIBE MOVEMENT — e.g. 'steam gently rising', 'leaves blowing in wind', 'water rippling']. Keep the main subject stable while [SPECIFIC ELEMENT] moves naturally. Duration: 4 seconds.
steps: Open Pika, select 'Image to Video'
Upload your Midjourney or Canva image
Describe the motion you want to add
Generate and download the animated clip
Add as an animated thumbnail preview or B-roll
